---
title: "Simulated Data Privacy Workflow"
subtitle: "Move structure and code, not sensitive records"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

# Goal

Practice a privacy-aware workflow where the AI works on simulated data and the final code is run on the real dataset outside the AI conversation.

# Core Principle

Do not send sensitive or regulated records to an external AI tool. Move structure, metadata, code, and simulated data instead.

# Paired Dataset Example

Real public teaching dataset:

```text
data/real/prams_2011_selected.csv
```

Simulated analog:

```text
data/simulated/new_parent_wellness_survey.csv
```

These files have matching columns but different topics and values.

# Workflow

1. Inspect the real dataset manually or with approved local tools.
2. Extract non-sensitive structure: column names, types, missingness patterns, and allowed values.
3. Create or choose a simulated dataset with the same structure.
4. Use AI tools only on the simulated dataset.
5. Develop cleaning, visualization, and modeling code on simulated data.
6. Review the code manually.
7. Run the reviewed code on the real dataset outside the AI tool.
8. Compare whether the code worked and what needed adjustment.

# Acceptance Criteria

Your notebook should include:

- A diagram or numbered description of the workflow.
- The real dataset path and simulated dataset path.
- A structure comparison showing matching column names.
- AI-assisted analysis code developed on the simulated data.
- A record of changes needed to run on the real data.
- A privacy reflection: what information did and did not leave the restricted side?

# Work Area

```{r}
# Start here.
```
